description: Executable inventory of product behaviors. Required entries must point to an existing test file and a unique source anchor; environment-gated entries must name the gate and runnable command.

behaviors.expectation: Every discovery surface advertises fusion-panel.
behaviors.anchor: model discovery doors advertise the fused model

behaviors.expectation: Every configured named ensemble is addressable as its own fused model.
Panel provider models remain available as native passthrough choices.
behaviors.anchor: must be advertised as a passthrough

behaviors.expectation: Claude model discovery uses the Anthropic model-list and single-model shapes.
Cursor-relative model discovery mirrors the canonical gateway catalog.
The real Claude CLI can select a named injected fused model and only its configured members run.
The real Codex CLI can select a named injected fused model and only its configured members run.
The real OpenCode CLI can select a named injected fused model and only its configured members run.
The real Cursor Agent can select fusion-panel through the Cursorkit bridge.
behaviors.gateReason: Requires a genuine Cursor login; the official binary is installed and version-checked in CI.

behaviors.expectation: k=1 panel members receive the caller's exact message history.
behaviors.anchor: members saw the caller's message verbatim

behaviors.expectation: k=1 panel members receive the caller's tool definitions on every front-door dialect.
behaviors.anchor: must be offered the caller's tools via

behaviors.expectation: Sampling, system prompts, auth, tools, and token limits are transformed according to each provider wire.
Cursor Responses-hybrid input is translated into a valid chat conversation without dropping tool history.
A fused panel turn fans out once to every healthy configured member.
Candidates are judged before synthesis, using the configured judge and synthesizer endpoints.
Selecting a named ensemble runs only its members and its judge.
Selecting a native provider model performs exactly one provider call and never invokes the judge.
Terminal fused responses carry synthesis decision, input trajectory ids, contribution diagnostics, and usage.
fusionkit/panel performs panel fanout, judge, and synthesis.
fusionkit/single calls only the default model.
fusionkit/self generates the configured number of samples and fuses them.
fusionkit/heuristic deterministically routes simple and hard requests to the documented modes.
Every provider client's out-of-band reasoning is parsed separately from answer text.
Each panel candidate's reasoning is preserved as trajectory evidence and reaches the judge prompt.
behaviors.anchor: candidate, judge, narration, and synthesizer reasoning survive fusion

behaviors.expectation: Structured judge analysis is visible on every door's supported reasoning channel.
The synthesizer model's own reasoning survives every gateway dialect.
behaviors.anchor: must carry the synthesizer model's own reasoning

behaviors.expectation: Panel fanout and judging progress are narrated without entering answer text.
behaviors.anchor: narrate panel progress on the reasoning channel

behaviors.expectation: Every door closes with its native terminal event or sentinel.
behaviors.anchor: fused streaming closes with the door's native marker

behaviors.expectation: The fused stream's terminal frame carries exact judge plus synthesizer usage.
Parallel tool calls packed into multi-slot chunks survive reassembly.
Truncated or garbage provider streams emit a safe error event and terminate rather than hanging.
Tool calls and results round-trip in the native vocabulary of every front door.
behaviors.anchor: multi-turn fused tool loop round-trips the door's tool dialect

behaviors.expectation: The actual Claude Code binary executes a committed Bash call and posts the result.
behaviors.anchor: real Claude Code CLI executes a fused Bash tool call

behaviors.expectation: The actual Codex binary executes a committed exec_command and posts the result.
behaviors.anchor: real Codex CLI executes a fused exec_command

behaviors.expectation: The actual OpenCode binary executes a committed bash call and posts the result.
behaviors.anchor: real OpenCode CLI executes a fused bash tool

behaviors.expectation: Finite k executes generations 1..k-1 and captures the k-th tool batch unexecuted.
behaviors.anchor: execute boundary 1 and capture boundary 2 unexecuted

behaviors.expectation: Unbounded managed worktree agents execute tools until a terminal model answer.
Claude Agent SDK and Codex SDK drivers use per-member native dialect gateways on multiple turns.
behaviors.anchor: real driver runs two fused turns

behaviors.expectation: Managed file tools reject worktree path traversal and record the failure as trajectory evidence.
A throttled native model fails over to fusion with the failed member excluded.
Passthrough policy surfaces the engine's classified provider failure verbatim.
Fail policy returns an explicit failure and never invokes fusion.
An over-budget session is refused with 402 before any provider call.
behaviors.anchor: over-budget session is refused before any model call

behaviors.expectation: Missing or incorrect gateway bearer credentials fail every door before provider spend.
behaviors.anchor: rejects every door before provider spend

behaviors.expectation: Finite-k rounds re-run the panel over updated messages.
Completed unbounded candidates persist across gateway restart and resume with zero re-fanout.
Fused usage and priced cost are persisted in the durable session ledger.
A slow k=1 sibling is abandoned after grace without delaying the survivor.
A hard panel timeout prevents judging partial work and does not poison the next turn.
Client cancellation prevents later judging and the gateway remains healthy.
Native runs create, complete, inspect, page events, and idempotently replay without extra provider calls.
The real FusionKit CLI loads config, preflights providers, spawns the Python engine, starts the gateway, and serves fusion.
The real serve-endpoint child process advertises and fronts its single configured model.
The real CLI covers config, prompts, install/uninstall, telemetry, setup, doctor, completions, versions, and runtime registry.
Fusion candidate, panel, judge, and provider spans use the registered semantic conventions and preserve trace context.
Local MLX model lifecycle, scale-to-zero, memory pressure, and OOM guidance work on Apple Silicon.
behaviors.gateReason: Requires Apple Silicon and downloaded MLX model weights.

behaviors.expectation: Real provider accounts accept the shipped request shapes and billed fusion completes.
behaviors.gateReason: Requires explicit provider credentials and incurs spend.
behaviors.liveCommand: uv run --package fusionkit fusionkit bench public-bench --help
